questions: Issue 2 ambiguity: 'no content shift when locking pane' — I'm interpreting this as 'the editor pane should NOT cause content to shift down when locked' (i.e., remove the paddingTop). Is that correct, or did you mean a different pane or a different behavior?
For issue 1 (unlock on navigate): should the pane also auto-lock when navigating AWAY from the editor page back to a tool page, or just unlock on arrival?

For issue 3: should the 70% apply only when the generations pane is *locked*, or also when it's merely *hover-open*? Current plan uses `generationsVisible` (locked OR open) to match the existing `generationsOffset` logic in EditorPaneTab.
success_criteria: Navigating to /tools/video-editor with editor pane locked → pane auto-unlocks (lock state persisted as false)
Locking the editor pane on any tool page does NOT add paddingTop to the main content area — content stays in place
When both editor and generations panes are locked/visible, editor pane height is 70% of viewport (not 90%)
When only editor pane is locked (generations not visible), editor pane height remains 90% of viewport
All existing tests in PanesContext.test.tsx, LayoutMainContent.test.tsx, and usePaneLockPolicyState.test.ts pass
assumptions: The editor pane is always position:fixed and should overlay content, never displace it — so paddingTop for editor lock is always wrong
The 70% target for dual-pane mode was chosen to leave ~30% for the generations pane default height (350px on typical viewports)
Unlocking on navigate means setting the persisted lock state to false (not just hiding the pane visually)
The getEffectivePaneHeights compression algorithm doesn't need changes — reducing the ideal from 90% to 70% is sufficient
